questions.question: What is the molecular modality of the API?
Is the API compatible with conventional oral absorption?
Is parenteral delivery inherently required for stability or bioavailability reasons?
Which route or dosage-form family is preferred?
What is the target patient population?
What is the dose burden per administration?
Is flexible dosing and/or easy swallowing required?
Is a swallowable solid dosage form acceptable for the intended population?
Are API flow and compressibility adequate for an oral solid route?
Is the API sufficiently stable in an aqueous liquid formulation?
How challenging is taste masking for an oral liquid?
Is local action on the skin or mucosal surface desired?
Can the API be engineered for inhaled delivery?
Is sterile injectable administration clinically appropriate?
Is skin permeability or patch-based delivery feasible?
Is direct compression or simple blending feasible for this product?
Is content uniformity manageable at the current drug-load category?
Are flow and compressibility adequate for conventional solid processing?
Is wet processing or liquid manufacture acceptable for this product?
Is the API stable during wet processing or liquid manufacture?
Is dose flexibility or liquid manufacture a key requirement?
Is aseptic or sterile processing feasible for this product?
Does the API benefit from gentle sterile processing due to heat or shear sensitivity?
Is the required cold-chain or preservative strategy acceptable?
Is specialized fabrication required for patch, topical, or inhaled delivery?
Are device, adhesion, or local-delivery performance requirements critical?
Is the API stable under specialized fabrication stresses such as heat, shear, or solvent exposure?
Is a more complex manufacturing route acceptable within project constraints?
Is a conventional immediate-release oral solid profile appropriate?
Is modified or prolonged release required?
Is a flexible-dose liquid profile preferred?
Is sterile systemic delivery required?
Is sustained transdermal skin delivery desired?
Is local topical delivery the main therapeutic goal?
Is pulmonary delivery the intended route?
Does the API require special protection from moisture, oxygen, light, or cold-chain stress?
Is additional product complexity acceptable to achieve protection or specialized handling?

possible_answers.comment: solid oral forms may be suitable mainly for older children with adequate swallowing ability
swallowing difficulties may limit conventional oral solids
liquid formulations support flexible dosing and easier administration
liquid formulations may help when swallowing is difficult
topicals can be appropriate depending on indication and exposure considerations
inhaled products may be appropriate but device handling can limit use in younger children
device coordination may reduce suitability
injectables may be clinically appropriate but are generally not preferred for routine pediatric administration
transdermal use is generally limited in pediatrics due to dosing and skin-permeability concerns
need for flexible dosing/easy swallowing argues against conventional oral solids
liquid formulations support easy swallowing and flexible dosing
may bypass swallowing issues depending on indication
device handling may offset swallowing advantage
injectables avoid swallowing but reduce convenience
transdermal delivery may support compliance in select cases
lack of swallowability excludes conventional oral solids
solid oral route may be suitable only for older children or selected patients
liquid manufacture/flexible dosing supports wet or liquid processing
if liquid manufacture is not needed, this route is less justified
liquid manufacture/flexible dosing supports wet or liquid processing
if liquid manufacture is not needed, this route is less justified
liquid manufacture/flexible dosing supports wet or liquid processing
if liquid manufacture is not needed, this route is less justified
conventional immediate-release oral solids are appropriate when swallowing and fixed dosing are acceptable
may still be feasible for older pediatric or selected patients
modified-release may be appropriate when prolonged exposure is required, but pediatric suitability should be assessed carefully
flexible-dose liquid profile is strongly compatible with pediatric use
